reasoning: There are currently no approved vaccines for human parainfluenza viruses (HPIV), nor are there any late-stage (Phase 3) trials nearing completion or regulatory review anywhere in the world. As of 2025, experimental vaccines (like Moderna's mRNA-1653 or various live-attenuated candidates) remain in early to mid-stage clinical trials (Phase 1 or 2). Given that vaccine approval typically requires successful Phase 3 trials and months of regulatory review, it is functionally impossible for a new vaccine to be approved and added to the Wikipedia list within the 1-3 month timeframe. The probabilities start at the minimum allowed bounds and only slightly account for the increasing base uncertainty over time (or Wikipedia vandalism/errors).
